title: Analog Devices Well-Placed For The Long Term, But Look To Be Opportunistic
text: Analog Devices' fiscal third quarter showed the first sequential revenue decline in years, with Industrial, Auto, and Communications all down from the prior quarter. Management guided to a worse-than-expected 12% sequential decline in Q4'23 revenue and believes it will take two to three quarters to work through excess inventories. While there are risks of a steeper trough cycle, including weakness in China, ADI has numerous attractive drivers across the next decade, including strong leverage to automation, digitalization, and electrification.

title: Don't Sleep on These 3 Quantum Computing Stocks That Will Mint Millionaires
text: Market volatility has struck again. Last week, U.S. equities continued their slide in August, leaving many market-watchers wondering whether or not equities in general had reached their peak.

title: Analog Devices (ADI) Q3 Earnings & Revenues Miss, Fall Y/Y
text: Analog Devices' (ADI) results for the fiscal third quarter reflect weakness across communications and consumer markets.

title: Analog Devices (ADI) Q3 Earnings: How Key Metrics Compare to Wall Street Estimates
text: The headline numbers for Analog Devices (ADI) give insight into how the company performed in the quarter ended July 2023, but it may be worthwhile to compare some of its key metrics to Wall Street estimates and the year-ago actuals.

title: Cramer warns: 'There are semis that aren't hitting it out of the park,' including this stock
text: Weakness in Analog Devices' consumer business contributed to missed quarterly results and light guidance.

title: Analog Devices (ADI) Q3 Earnings and Revenues Lag Estimates
text: Analog Devices (ADI) came out with quarterly earnings of $2.49 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $2.53 per share. This compares to earnings of $2.52 per share a year ago.

title: Analog Devices fails to match Nvidia as sales flatline
text: Analog Devices Inc shares fell 6.6% as the semiconductor continued to struggle to match industry rival, Nvidia Inc, which reports results after the closing bell Wednesday. The Wilmington, Massachusetts-based firm reported a 1% dip in sales in the financial third quarter to July 29 to $3.08 billion from $3.11 billion.

title: Analog Devices Stock Sinks on Weak Outlook. It's Bad News for Chip Stocks.
text: Analog Devices shares dropped on Wednesday after its quarterly guidance missed analysts' expectations. Other semiconductor companies with big industrial and automotive exposure were also falling.

title: Analog Devices stock slides as earnings fall short and company says it faces ‘near-term turbulence.'
text: Analog Devices Inc. stock ADI, -0.32% was down nearly 8% in premarket trades Wednesday after the chip maker's adjusted third-quarter profit missed Wall Street estimates. Analog Devices said its profit for the three months ended July 29 rose 4% to $929 million, or $1.74 a share, from $893 million, or $1.44 a share in the year-ago period.

title: Chipmaker Analog Devices Misses Targets As Inventories Remain High
text: Chipmaker Analog Devices missed Wall Street's targets for its fiscal third quarter as customers work through high inventories.

title: Analog Devices Reports Third Quarter Fiscal 2023 Financial Results
text: WILMINGTON, Mass.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Analog Devices, Inc. (Nasdaq: ADI), a global semiconductor leader, today announced financial results for its third quarter fiscal year 2023, which ended July 29, 2023. “In a challenging operating environment, ADI executed well, and delivered third quarter results within our expectations. However, the customer inventory adjustments we mentioned last quarter have accelerated as economic conditions deteriorate and our lead times continue to improve,” said Vincen.

title: Analog Devices: Slightly Expensive For My Risk Profile
text: Analysts predict low revenue growth for Analog Devices in the coming years, dampening the outlook for the company. The semiconductor sector has been impacted by high inventory levels and low demand, but is expected to rebound strongly in the next year. Analog Devices' financials show manageable debt, improving profitability, and efficiency metrics, positioning the company well for future economic downturns.
